acceptance_criteria: When a project prompt contains the bead_execution_prompt contract marker, the plan_and_implement stage prompt includes scope-enforcement guidance that references Must-Do Scope, Explicit Non-Goals, and Already Planned Elsewhere sections by name
The scope guidance instructs the LLM to treat Must-Do Scope + Acceptance Criteria as the implementation boundary and to not implement work listed under Explicit Non-Goals or Already Planned Elsewhere

The scope guidance instructs the LLM to record any out-of-scope discoveries (bugs, improvements, related work) as deferred items in the outstanding_risks field with a clear 'DEFERRED:' prefix or equivalent structured marker
When a project prompt does NOT contain the contract marker, the plan_and_implement stage prompt is unchanged from current behavior (empty classification_guidance block, no scope enforcement injected)
The scope guidance references Milestone Summary for context but explicitly states this context must not expand the implementation boundary beyond the current bead
Existing tests continue to pass; new tests verify scope guidance is injected for contract-bearing prompts and absent for non-contract prompts
The guidance text is generated by a dedicated function (not inlined in engine.rs) following the pattern established by render_classification_guidance in review_classification.rs
constraints: Must use the existing template placeholder mechanism (classification_guidance or a new placeholder) rather than modifying the project prompt itself
Must follow the established pattern in engine.rs lines 166-171 where guidance injection is conditional on stage_id and contract presence
Must not require changes to the bead task prompt contract format or version (contract_version remains 1)
The guidance rendering function should live in task_prompt_contract.rs or a new sibling module, not in engine.rs directly
Must work correctly for both minimal flow (PlanAndImplement + FinalReview) and quick_dev flow where PlanAndImplement is the execution stage
goals: Inject scope-enforcement guidance into the plan_and_implement stage prompt that instructs the LLM to constrain its plan and implementation strictly to Must-Do Scope and Acceptance Criteria boundaries
Add structured 'deferred items' output to the plan_and_implement stage so out-of-scope work discovered during planning is captured rather than silently implemented or ignored
Surface Explicit Non-Goals and Already Planned Elsewhere sections as hard boundaries the implementer must not cross, with explicit instruction to note related discoveries as deferred
Include milestone context (Milestone Summary) in the scope guidance so the implementer understands the broader context without expanding scope beyond the current bead
Maintain full backward compatibility: when no bead task prompt contract marker is present (non-milestone mode), the stage behaves exactly as it does today with no additional guidance injected
non_goals: Adding classification guidance (fix-now / planned-elsewhere / propose-new-bead) to plan_and_implement—that pattern is specific to review stages and not appropriate for the implementer
Changing the ExecutionPayload schema or adding new top-level fields beyond what's needed for deferred items
Modifying the review or final_review stage scope handling—those already have classification guidance from commits #123-#126
Adding new milestone or bead metadata loading infrastructure—the existing task_prompt_contract sections already carry all needed context
Changing how the project prompt is generated or how bead task prompt contracts are structured
problem_summary: The plan_and_implement stage currently receives the bead task prompt contract sections (Must-Do Scope, Explicit Non-Goals, Already Planned Elsewhere, Milestone Summary) via the project prompt, and gets generic consumer guidance telling it to treat those sections as authoritative. However, it lacks the structured scope-enforcement guidance that review and final_review stages received in commits #123-#126. This means the implementer LLM may drift outside bead scope—touching code covered by other beads, implementing non-goals, or missing scope boundaries—without any structured mechanism to flag out-of-scope work as deferred. The plan_and_implement stage needs scope-aware guidance injected into its prompt so it actively constrains its plan and implementation to the bead's declared scope, notes out-of-scope discoveries as deferred items, and remains backward compatible when no milestone/bead context is present.
risks_or_open_questions: Should deferred items use the existing outstanding_risks field in ExecutionPayload (with a prefix convention like 'DEFERRED:') or should a new deferred_items field be added to the payload schema? Using outstanding_risks is simpler and backward compatible but less structured.
Should the scope guidance also be injected into the Plan stage (StageId::Plan) when used in standard flow, or only PlanAndImplement? The Plan stage faces the same scope drift risk.

How assertive should the guidance be? Too rigid may cause the LLM to skip genuinely necessary prerequisite work (e.g., a refactor needed to implement the scoped feature). The guidance should allow 'strictly necessary enabling work' while flagging it.
The consumer_guidance_body() already mentions scope boundaries generically—need to ensure the new plan_and_implement-specific guidance complements rather than contradicts or duplicates it.
